judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable depth and accuracy; Report 1 is marginally stronger on the legacy secret (explicitly calling for removal) while Report 2 is marginally stronger on the shadow-sink fix (explicit empty-string value), making them roughly equivalent overall.

report_1.evidence: Line 2 `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` — developer debug tooling must not run in prod... Line 3 `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` — relaxed authentication... Line 4 `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` — a test account with superadmin privileges... Line 7 `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"` — a legacy secret reference carried over from staging

report_2.evidence: Line 2, `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` — enables the debug toolbar in production... Line 3, `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` — relaxed authentication... Line 4, `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` — a preset QA superadmin account would exist in production... Line 6, `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ...` The sink must be set to "" ... Line 7, `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = ...`

report_1.evidence: VITE_-prefixed variables are inlined into the bundle at build time via import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL... the deploy-prod job (deploy.yml:29-35) deploys the same already-built digest to prod. So the production frontend would ship with the staging API URL baked in... There is no frontend/.env.production in the repo and no prod build step; both are needed (rebuild per environment rather than promoting the digest...)

report_2.evidence: Because the build runs with --mode staging, Vite loads frontend/.env.staging, which sets VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com. There is no .env.production in the repo. Result: the production deployment will ship a bundle hard-wired to the staging API. Either a frontend/.env.production plus a separate production build is needed, or the app must read the API URL at runtime instead of build time.

report_1.evidence: No migration step: docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in deploy.yml (nor any script, since deploy.sh is absent) runs the three migrations in backend/migrations/versions/... on the first prod deploy the schema (0001_base, 0002_tenants, 0003_billing) would need a manual first pass.

report_2.evidence: the runbook (docs/prod-runbook.md, "last updated: 3 months ago") claims migrations are applied automatically during deploy, but the mechanism would be inside the missing deploy.sh — nothing in the workflow runs the three migrations in backend/migrations/versions/... For a first launch, the production database needs all three applied from scratch, and nothing verifiable does that.

report_1.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — the prod deploy role trusts the staging GitHub environment. Change to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production.

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf line 3 was copied unedited and still says oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging". So the production deploy role trusts only staging-environment tokens... staging-environment jobs would be trusted by the production role — an environment-separation violation.
